controls.title: Account Management
controls.implementationSummary: This control ensures accounts are authorized, provisioned, changed, and removed through controlled lifecycle workflows. The platform supports that objective through organization policy controls, SCIM-backed provisioning paths, role-based boundaries, and auditable membership lifecycle events in the hosted service.
controls.controlStatement: Define and document the types of accounts allowed and specifically prohibited for use within the system; Assign account managers; Require {{ insert: param, ac-02_odp.01 }} for group and role membership; Specify: Authorized users of the system; Group and role membership; and Access authorizations (i.e., privileges) and {{ insert: param, ac-02_odp.02 }} for each account; Require approvals by {{ insert: param, ac-02_odp.03 }} for requests to create accounts; Create, enable, modify, disable, and remove accounts in accordance with {{ insert: param, ac-02_odp.04 }}; Monitor the use of accounts; Notify account managers and {{ insert: param, ac-02_odp.05 }} within: {{ insert: param, ac-02_odp.06 }} when accounts are no longer required; {{ insert: param, ac-02_odp.07 }} when users are terminated or transferred; and {{ insert: param, ac-02_odp.08 }} when system usage or need-to-know changes for an individual; Authorize access to the system based on: A valid access authorization; Intended system usage; and {{ insert: param, ac-02_odp.09 }}; Review accounts for compliance with account management requirements {{ insert: param, ac-02_odp.10 }}; Establish and implement a process for changing shared or group account authenticators (if deployed) when individuals are removed from the group; and Align account management processes with personnel termination and transfer processes.

platformChecklistItems.label: Role boundaries are enforced
platformChecklistItems.description: Account roles and tenant boundaries must prevent unauthorized cross-account access.
platformChecklistItems.verificationMethod: Authorization test coverage and admin workflow inspection

seed.notes: Member role changes validate organization membership and require site-admin write access before mutation.

evidence.title: Site-admin member role mutation guard
evidence.description: convex/auth.ts enforces assertSiteAdminWriteAccess before member role updates.

platformChecklistItems.label: Account lifecycle controls are implemented
platformChecklistItems.description: Invitations, provisioning, suspension, deactivation, and reactivation workflows must support controlled onboarding and offboarding.
platformChecklistItems.verificationMethod: Organization management and provisioning workflow review

seed.notes: Organization policies constrain invitations and seat growth, while SCIM and member-state workflows support lifecycle changes.

evidence.title: Organization access policy controls
evidence.description: convex/organizationManagement.ts enforces invitePolicy, verifiedDomainsOnly, and memberCap policy checks; src/features/organizations/components/OrganizationPoliciesCard.tsx exposes the management surface.

evidence.title: SCIM provisioning workflow
evidence.description: src/features/organizations/components/OrganizationProvisioningManagement.tsx and src/features/organizations/server/organization-management.ts manage SCIM endpoint and bearer-token lifecycle for automated provisioning.

platformChecklistItems.label: Membership changes are auditable
platformChecklistItems.description: Administrative membership changes must emit reviewable audit records.

seed.notes: Membership lifecycle events are emitted as audit events and reviewable through organization audit history.

evidence.title: Organization membership audit events
evidence.description: src/lib/shared/auth-audit.ts defines member_added, member_removed, member_role_updated, member_suspended, member_deactivated, member_reactivated, and SCIM lifecycle event types.

evidence.title: Organization audit review surface
evidence.description: src/features/organizations/components/OrganizationAuditPage.tsx exposes membership-related audit history to organization owners, admins, and site admins.

controls.customerResponsibilityNotes: Customer organizations are responsible for workforce onboarding, termination, role assignment approval, and periodic access review.

nist80066.label: HIPAA Standard: Implement policies and procedures to ensure that all members of its workforce have appropriate access to electronic protected health information, as provided under paragraph (a)(4) of this section, and to prevent those workforce members who do not have access under paragraph (a)(4) of this section from obtaining access to electronic protected health information.
HIPAA Standard: Implement policies and procedures for authorizing access to electronic protected health information that are consistent with the applicable requirements of subpart E of this part.

csf20.label: PR / PR.AA: Identities and credentials for authorized users, services, and hardware are managed by the organization
PR / PR.AA: Access permissions, entitlements, and authorizations are defined in a policy, managed, enforced, and reviewed, and incorporate the principles of least privilege and separation of duties
PR / PR.DS: The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-in-use are protected
DE / DE.CM: Networks and network services are monitored to find potentially adverse events
DE / DE.CM: Personnel activity and technology usage are monitored to find potentially adverse events

soc2.label: The entity implements logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures over protected information assets to protect them from security events to meet the entity's objectives.

Prior to issuing system credentials and granting system access, the entity registers and authorizes new internal and external users whose access is administered by the entity. For those users whose access is administered by the entity, user system credentials are removed when user access is no longer authorized.

The entity monitors system components and the operation of those components for anomalies that are indicative of malicious acts, natural disasters, and errors affecting the entity's ability to meet its objectives; anomalies are analyzed to determine whether they represent security events.
The entity responds to identified security incidents by executing a defined incident response program to understand, contain, remediate, and communicate security incidents, as appropriate.

controls.title: Access Enforcement
controls.implementationSummary: This control ensures users and processes can perform only the actions and data access they are authorized to use. The platform addresses that objective through route-level guards, server-side authorization checks, and organization-scoped permission decisions for protected application flows and sensitive operations.
controls.controlStatement: Access Enforcement is tracked as an active control in the platform register.

platformChecklistItems.label: Protected routes are guarded
platformChecklistItems.description: Protected pages must require authorized access before rendering sensitive content.
platformChecklistItems.verificationMethod: Route guard review and route-level test coverage

seed.notes: Protected routes are guarded through shared auth and admin guard utilities.

evidence.title: Shared route guards
evidence.description: src/features/auth/server/route-guards.ts and auth-guards.ts provide requireAuth and requireAdmin guard paths used across protected flows.

platformChecklistItems.label: Server authorization checks enforce access
platformChecklistItems.description: Server-side actions and data access must enforce role-appropriate authorization.
platformChecklistItems.verificationMethod: Server function inspection and automated tests

seed.notes: Server-side access checks rely on requireAuth and requireAdmin before sensitive actions.

evidence.title: Server auth guard enforcement
evidence.description: src/features/auth/server/auth-guards.ts implements requireAuth and requireAdmin for protected server operations.

platformChecklistItems.label: Organization-scoped permissions restrict actions
platformChecklistItems.description: Organization membership, role, and policy context must constrain who can manage organization resources.
platformChecklistItems.verificationMethod: Organization access context and permission helper review

seed.notes: Organization access is evaluated against viewer role, membership state, and policy gates before privileged actions are allowed.

evidence.title: Organization permission helpers
evidence.description: src/features/organizations/lib/organization-permissions.ts defines organization capability rules used across directory and policy workflows.

evidence.title: Organization access enforcement
evidence.description: convex/organizationManagement.ts resolves organization access context and rejects unauthorized member, policy, and provisioning mutations.

controls.customerResponsibilityNotes: Customer organizations are responsible for defining roles, approving privileged access, and maintaining least-privilege assignments within the service.

nist80066.label: HIPAA Standard: Implement policies and procedures for authorizing access to electronic protected health information that are consistent with the applicable requirements of subpart E of this part.

csf20.label: PR / PR.AA: Access permissions, entitlements, and authorizations are defined in a policy, managed, enforced, and reviewed, and incorporate the principles of least privilege and separation of duties
PR / PR.DS: The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-in-use are protected
PR / PR.IR: Networks and environments are protected from unauthorized logical access and usage

soc2.label: The entity implements logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures over protected information assets to protect them from security events to meet the entity's objectives.

The entity authorizes, modifies, or removes access to data, software, functions, and other protected information assets based on roles, responsibilities, or the system design and changes, giving consideration to the concepts of least privilege and segregation of duties, to meet the entity’s objectives.

The entity monitors system components and the operation of those components for anomalies that are indicative of malicious acts, natural disasters, and errors affecting the entity's ability to meet its objectives; anomalies are analyzed to determine whether they represent security events.

The entity grants identified and authenticated data subjects the ability to access their stored personal information for review and, upon request, provides physical or electronic copies of that information to data subjects to meet the entity’s objectives related to privacy. If access is denied, data subjects are informed of the denial and reason for such denial, as required, to meet the entity’s objectives related to privacy.

controls.title: Event Logging
controls.implementationSummary: This control ensures security-relevant events are identified, recorded, protected, and made available for oversight. The platform addresses that objective by capturing audit events for authentication, administrative, and security-significant activity, integrity-linking those records, and exposing them for review and export.
controls.controlStatement: Identify the types of events that the system is capable of logging in support of the audit function: {{ insert: param, au-02_odp.01 }}; Coordinate the event logging function with other organizational entities requiring audit-related information to guide and inform the selection criteria for events to be logged; Specify the following event types for logging within the system: {{ insert: param, au-2_prm_2 }}; Provide a rationale for why the event types selected for logging are deemed to be adequate to support after-the-fact investigations of incidents; and Review and update the event types selected for logging {{ insert: param, au-02_odp.04 }}.

platformChecklistItems.label: Security-relevant events are captured
platformChecklistItems.description: Authentication, administrative, and security-significant events must be recorded.

seed.notes: Audit event types cover authentication, organization, attachment, and evidence-report activity.

evidence.title: Audit event inventory
evidence.description: src/lib/shared/auth-audit.ts defines a broad set of security-relevant audit event types.

platformChecklistItems.label: Audit records can be reviewed and exported
platformChecklistItems.description: Authorized reviewers must be able to inspect and export audit evidence.

seed.notes: The platform exposes audit review and evidence export surfaces for security review.

evidence.title: Organization audit review UI
evidence.description: src/features/organizations/components/OrganizationAuditPage.tsx provides audit history review and export flows.

evidence.title: Security evidence export workflow
evidence.description: src/routes/app/admin/security.tsx and convex/security.ts provide evidence report generation and export flows.

platformChecklistItems.label: Audit record integrity is checked
platformChecklistItems.description: Audit records should retain integrity metadata or verification signals that help detect tampering.

seed.notes: Audit records are hash-linked and the platform can detect and log integrity failures.

evidence.title: Hash-linked audit log chain
evidence.description: convex/audit.ts stores eventHash and previousEventHash for audit events and verifies the chain during integrity checks.

controls.customerResponsibilityNotes: Customer organizations are responsible for reviewing exported audit records and aligning retention or downstream log handling with their own policies when those records leave the platform.

nist80066.label: Implement procedures to regularly review records of information system activity, such as audit logs, access reports, and security incident tracking reports.
HIPAA Standard: Implement hardware, software, and/or procedural mechanisms that record and examine activity in information systems that contain or use electronic protected health information.

csf20.label: PR / PR.PS: Log records are generated and made available for continuous monitoring

soc2.label: The entity monitors system components and the operation of those components for anomalies that are indicative of malicious acts, natural disasters, and errors affecting the entity's ability to meet its objectives; anomalies are analyzed to determine whether they represent security events.

controls.title: Identification and Authentication (Organizational Users)
controls.implementationSummary: This control ensures users are uniquely identified and authenticated before accessing protected service functionality. The platform supports that objective through authenticated access flows, verified-email enforcement, MFA or passkey enforcement for regulated access, and fresh-session step-up for sensitive operations.
controls.controlStatement: Identification and Authentication (Organizational Users) is tracked as an active control in the platform register.

platformChecklistItems.label: Authenticated access is required
platformChecklistItems.description: The platform must require authenticated access for protected user areas.

seed.notes: Protected application use depends on authenticated access flows.

evidence.title: Shared authenticated route enforcement
evidence.description: src/features/auth/server/auth-guards.ts and route-guards.ts gate authenticated and admin application access.

platformChecklistItems.label: Verified email checks are enforced
platformChecklistItems.description: Production authentication flows must enforce verified-email expectations where required.
platformChecklistItems.verificationMethod: Policy inspection and auth configuration review

seed.notes: Verified email is enforced before protected access and is part of the regulated baseline.

evidence.title: Verified email enforcement
evidence.description: src/features/auth/server/auth-guards.ts redirects unverified accounts away from protected access and src/lib/shared/security-baseline.ts requires verified email by default.

platformChecklistItems.label: MFA or passkeys are enforced for regulated access
platformChecklistItems.description: The platform must enforce stronger authenticators for privileged or regulated organization access paths.
platformChecklistItems.verificationMethod: Authentication policy and step-up workflow review

seed.notes: The regulated baseline requires MFA or passkeys, and privileged access paths enforce that requirement.

evidence.title: Regulated MFA baseline
evidence.description: src/lib/shared/security-baseline.ts requires MFA or passkeys for regulated organizations and src/features/organizations/components/OrganizationPoliciesCard.tsx presents the always-enforced posture.

evidence.title: MFA and fresh-session enforcement
evidence.description: src/features/auth/server/auth-guards.ts and convex/organizationManagement.ts require MFA/passkey presence or a fresh session before privileged access and regulated join flows.

controls.customerResponsibilityNotes: Customer organizations are responsible for identity proofing, MFA policy decisions, user enrollment expectations, and account lifecycle governance.

nist80066.label: Assign a unique name and/or number for identifying and tracking user identity.
HIPAA Standard: Implement procedures to verify that a person or entity seeking access to electronic protected health information is the one claimed.

csf20.label: PR / PR.AA: Identities and credentials for authorized users, services, and hardware are managed by the organization
PR / PR.AA: Users, services, and hardware are authenticated

soc2.label: The entity implements logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures over protected information assets to protect them from security events to meet the entity's objectives.

controls.title: Protection of Information at Rest
controls.implementationSummary: This control ensures information stored within the service boundary is protected against unauthorized access or alteration at rest. The platform addresses that objective through managed encrypted storage, blocked public access, controlled file serving, and retention behavior for data managed within the service boundary.
controls.controlStatement: Protection of Information at Rest is tracked as an active control in the platform register.

platformChecklistItems.label: Hosted storage protections are configured
platformChecklistItems.description: Data at rest protections must be configured for hosted storage within the service boundary.

seed.notes: Managed storage configuration enforces blocked public access, server-side encryption, versioning, and signed file-serving paths.

evidence.title: Encrypted S3 storage configuration
evidence.description: infra/aws-cdk/lib/malware-scan-stack.cts provisions an S3 bucket with BLOCK_ALL public access, S3-managed encryption, enforceSSL, object ownership enforcement, and versioning.

evidence.title: Controlled file-serving paths
evidence.description: convex/storagePlatform.ts resolves signed file URLs and routes non-Convex storage access through signed serve paths.

platformChecklistItems.label: Retention behavior is implemented
platformChecklistItems.description: Retention jobs or controls must enforce data handling expectations.

seed.notes: Retention jobs are tracked and surfaced in the security posture summary.

evidence.title: Retention job tracking
evidence.description: convex/security.ts reports retention job status and convex/schema.ts defines retentionJobs.

controls.customerResponsibilityNotes: Customer organizations are responsible for governing exported data, retention obligations they impose on platform use, and any external storage or integrations they control.

nist80066.label: Consider whether the addressable implementation specifications of this standard are reasonable and appropriate:

csf20.label: PR / PR.DS: The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-at-rest are protected

soc2.label: The entity implements logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures over protected information assets to protect them from security events to meet the entity's objectives.

controls.title: System Backup
controls.implementationSummary: This control ensures service data and required system information are backed up, protected, and recoverable after disruption or loss. For the hosted service, that means operator-run backup and restore capability for the production environment; this workspace currently evidences backup-verification recordkeeping, but operator backup configuration and restore-test evidence are not yet fully attached here.
controls.controlStatement: Conduct backups of user-level information contained in {{ insert: param, cp-09_odp.01 }} {{ insert: param, cp-09_odp.02 }}; Conduct backups of system-level information contained in the system {{ insert: param, cp-09_odp.03 }}; Conduct backups of system documentation, including security- and privacy-related documentation {{ insert: param, cp-09_odp.04 }} ; and Protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of backup information.

platformChecklistItems.label: Hosted service backups are configured
platformChecklistItems.description: Provider-operated backups for production service data and essential system information should be defined and enabled.

seed.notes: The control register does not yet include provider backup configuration evidence for the hosted production environment.

platformChecklistItems.label: Backup verification records are maintained
platformChecklistItems.description: The platform should retain verification outcomes or supporting records for completed backup checks and recovery validation.

seed.notes: The platform includes a backup verification report table and internal mutation for recording backup verification outcomes.

evidence.title: Backup verification storage
evidence.description: convex/schema.ts defines backupVerificationReports and convex/security.ts exposes recordBackupVerification.

platformChecklistItems.label: Restore testing is performed and recorded
platformChecklistItems.description: Provider-operated restore tests and recovery validation results should be documented for the hosted service.

seed.notes: The control workspace does not yet contain restore test results or recovery exercise records for the hosted production environment.

controls.customerResponsibilityNotes: Customer organizations are responsible for determining whether the hosted service backup and recovery posture satisfies their retention, recovery-time, and business continuity requirements, and for protecting any data they export or replicate outside the service.

nist80066.label: Establish and implement procedures to create and maintain retrievable exact copies of ePHI.
Establish (and implement as needed) procedures to restore any loss of data.

csf20.label: PR / PR.DS: The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-at-rest are protected
PR / PR.DS: The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-in-use are protected
PR / PR.DS: Backups of data are created, protected, maintained, and tested
RC / RC.RP: The integrity of backups and other restoration assets is verified before using them for restoration

soc2.label: The entity authorizes, designs, develops or acquires, implements, operates, approves, maintains, and monitors environmental protections, software, data back-up processes, and recovery infrastructure to meet its objectives.
The entity tests recovery plan procedures supporting system recovery to meet its objectives.
The entity implements logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures over protected information assets to protect them from security events to meet the entity's objectives.

controls.title: Vulnerability Monitoring and Scanning
controls.implementationSummary: This control ensures vulnerabilities or security-relevant findings affecting the hosted service are identified, assessed, remediated, or formally risk-accepted. The app ships automated inspection and malware-finding hooks for file-ingest surfaces, but hosted-service vulnerability scanning cadence and remediation tracking still require operator processes.
controls.controlStatement: Monitor and scan for vulnerabilities in the system and hosted applications {{ insert: param, ra-5_prm_1 }} and when new vulnerabilities potentially affecting the system are identified and reported; Employ vulnerability monitoring tools and techniques that facilitate interoperability among tools and automate parts of the vulnerability management process by using standards for: Enumerating platforms, software flaws, and improper configurations; Formatting checklists and test procedures; and Measuring vulnerability impact; Analyze vulnerability scan reports and results from vulnerability monitoring; Remediate legitimate vulnerabilities {{ insert: param, ra-05_odp.03 }} in accordance with an organizational assessment of risk; Share information obtained from the vulnerability monitoring process and control assessments with {{ insert: param, ra-05_odp.04 }} to help eliminate similar vulnerabilities in other systems; and Employ vulnerability monitoring tools that include the capability to readily update the vulnerabilities to be scanned.

platformChecklistItems.label: Automated security scanning hooks exist
platformChecklistItems.description: The platform should implement automated inspection or malware-finding hooks for security-relevant ingest paths.

seed.notes: The platform has built-in file inspection and GuardDuty webhook integration for file-ingest surfaces, but that is not full hosted-service vulnerability-management evidence.

evidence.title: Built-in file inspection
evidence.description: src/lib/server/file-inspection.server.ts inspects file signatures, types, and size limits before files proceed through document workflows.

evidence.title: GuardDuty malware finding pipeline
evidence.description: infra/aws-cdk/lib/malware-scan-stack.cts provisions S3 malware scanning and convex/storageWebhook.ts verifies signed findings before quarantining affected files.

platformChecklistItems.label: Remediation and risk treatment are tracked
platformChecklistItems.description: The platform must retain remediation tracking or risk acceptance artifacts.

seed.notes: No remediation-tracking or risk-acceptance artifact is attached from the codebase.

controls.customerResponsibilityNotes: Customer organizations are responsible for addressing vulnerabilities in their own endpoints, identity stores, integrations, and operational environments connected to the service.

nist80066.label: Conduct an accurate and thorough assessment of the potential risks and vulnerabilities to the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of ePHI held by the covered entity or business associate.
Implement security measures sufficient to reduce risks and vulnerabilities to a reasonable and appropriate level to comply with §164.306(a)

csf20.label: GV / GV.SC: Cybersecurity supply chain risk management plans include provisions for activities that occur after the conclusion of a partnership or service agreement
ID / ID.RA: Vulnerabilities in assets are identified, validated, and recorded
ID / ID.RA: Processes for receiving, analyzing, and responding to vulnerability disclosures are established
ID / ID.IM: Improvements are identified from evaluations
ID / ID.IM: Improvements are identified from security tests and exercises, including those done in coordination with suppliers and relevant third parties
ID / ID.IM: Improvements are identified from execution of operational processes, procedures, and activities

soc2.label: To meet its objectives, the entity uses detection and monitoring procedures to identify (1) changes to configurations that result in the introduction of new vulnerabilities, and (2) susceptibilities to newly discovered vulnerabilities.

The entity monitors system components and the operation of those components for anomalies that are indicative of malicious acts, natural disasters, and errors affecting the entity's ability to meet its objectives; anomalies are analyzed to determine whether they represent security events.

controls.title: Transmission Confidentiality and Integrity
controls.implementationSummary: This control ensures information transmitted by the service is protected against unauthorized disclosure or modification in transit. The platform addresses that objective by enforcing HTTPS-oriented auth configuration, trusted-origin checks, secure cookie behavior, and SSL-only storage transport for managed file paths.
controls.controlStatement: Transmission Confidentiality and Integrity is tracked as an active control in the platform register.

platformChecklistItems.label: HTTPS-oriented endpoint configuration is enforced
platformChecklistItems.description: Production auth configuration and trusted origins must require secure transport for hosted endpoints.
platformChecklistItems.verificationMethod: Auth URL and origin configuration review

seed.notes: Better Auth configuration fails closed unless production origins use HTTPS and trusted origins are explicitly allowed.

evidence.title: HTTPS Better Auth configuration
evidence.description: src/lib/server/env.server.ts requires BETTER_AUTH_URL and trusted origins to use HTTPS unless they point to loopback development.

platformChecklistItems.label: Secure session transport settings are enforced
platformChecklistItems.description: Session and temporary-link transport settings must align with secure transport expectations.

seed.notes: Short-lived verification windows and temporary-link TTLs are surfaced in the security posture summary.

evidence.title: Session transport configuration
evidence.description: convex/betterAuth/sharedOptions.ts enables secure cookies for HTTPS origins and convex/security.ts reports sessionExpiryHours, freshWindowMinutes, and temporaryLinkTtlMinutes.

platformChecklistItems.label: Certificate and edge operations are documented
platformChecklistItems.description: The hosted platform must retain evidence for certificate lifecycle management and edge enforcement.

seed.notes: Certificate lifecycle management and edge enforcement artifacts are not stored in the repo-backed control workspace yet.

controls.customerResponsibilityNotes: Customer organizations are responsible for requiring secure access to the service within their own networks, browsers, devices, and downstream integrations.

nist80066.label: HIPAA Standard: Implement technical security measures to guard against unauthorized access to electronic protected health information that is being transmitted over an electronic communications network.
Implement security measures to ensure that electronically transmitted ePHI is not improperly modified without detection until disposed of.

csf20.label: PR / PR.DS: The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-in-transit are protected

soc2.label: The entity restricts the transmission, movement, and removal of information to authorized internal and external users and processes, and protects it during transmission, movement, or removal to meet the entity’s objectives.

controls.title: Incident Handling
controls.implementationSummary: This control ensures security incidents can be handled through defined response, investigation, and follow-up procedures. The platform supports that objective by providing audit trails, exportable evidence, and retained investigation artifacts that can assist incident investigation and post-incident analysis, while substantive incident response procedures remain customer-operated in this model.
controls.controlStatement: Implement an incident handling capability for incidents that is consistent with the incident response plan and includes preparation, detection and analysis, containment, eradication, and recovery; Coordinate incident handling activities with contingency planning activities; Incorporate lessons learned from ongoing incident handling activities into incident response procedures, training, and testing, and implement the resulting changes accordingly; and Ensure the rigor, intensity, scope, and results of incident handling activities are comparable and predictable across the organization.

platformChecklistItems.label: Incident-supporting evidence can be exported
platformChecklistItems.description: The platform should expose audit trails and evidence exports that support investigations.

seed.notes: Audit and evidence export flows can support incident investigations and post-incident analysis.

evidence.title: Evidence export support
evidence.description: src/routes/app/admin/security.tsx and convex/security.ts support evidence report generation and export.

platformChecklistItems.label: Investigation-supporting artifacts can be retained
platformChecklistItems.description: The platform should retain hashes, review state, and exported evidence metadata that support post-incident analysis.

seed.notes: Evidence reports persist review metadata and integrity-linked export details that can be used during investigations.

evidence.title: Integrity-linked evidence reports
evidence.description: convex/security.ts stores contentHash, exportHash, exportIntegritySummary, and review metadata for generated evidence reports.

controls.customerResponsibilityNotes: Customer organizations are responsible for incident response procedures, internal escalation contacts, and customer-side post-incident handling.

nist80066.label: HIPAA Standard: Implement policies and procedures to address security incidents.

csf20.label: ID / ID.IM: Improvements are identified from evaluations
ID / ID.IM: Improvements are identified from security tests and exercises, including those done in coordination with suppliers and relevant third parties
ID / ID.IM: Improvements are identified from execution of operational processes, procedures, and activities
DE / DE.AE: Potentially adverse events are analyzed to better understand associated activities
DE / DE.AE: Information is correlated from multiple sources
DE / DE.AE: Information on adverse events is provided to authorized staff and tools
DE / DE.AE: Incidents are declared when adverse events meet the defined incident criteria
RS / RS.MA: Incident reports are triaged and validated
RS / RS.MA: Incidents are categorized and prioritized
RS / RS.MA: Incidents are escalated or elevated as needed
RS / RS.MA: The criteria for initiating incident recovery are applied
RS / RS.AN: Analysis is performed to establish what has taken place during an incident and the root cause of the incident
RS / RS.AN: Actions performed during an investigation are recorded, and the records' integrity and provenance are preserved
RS / RS.AN: Incident data and metadata are collected, and their integrity and provenance are preserved
RS / RS.AN: An incident's magnitude is estimated and validated
RS / RS.CO: Internal and external stakeholders are notified of incidents
RS / RS.CO: Information is shared with designated internal and external stakeholders
RS / RS.MI: Incidents are contained
RS / RS.MI: Incidents are eradicated
RC / RC.RP: The recovery portion of the incident response plan is executed once initiated from the incident response process
RC / RC.RP: Recovery actions are selected, scoped, prioritized, and performed
RC / RC.RP: The end of incident recovery is declared based on criteria, and incident-related documentation is completed
RC / RC.CO: Recovery activities and progress in restoring operational capabilities are communicated to designated internal and external stakeholders
RC / RC.CO: Public updates on incident recovery are shared using approved methods and messaging

soc2.label: The entity responds to identified security incidents by executing a defined incident response program to understand, contain, remediate, and communicate security incidents, as appropriate.
The entity identifies, develops, and implements activities to recover from identified security incidents.

controls.title: Authenticator Management
controls.implementationSummary: This control ensures authenticators are managed, protected, and recoverable in a controlled manner. The platform supports that objective through stronger authenticators, recovery-related auditing, and guarded reset or account-recovery flows.
controls.controlStatement: Verifying, as part of the initial authenticator distribution, the identity of the individual, group, role, service, or device receiving the authenticator; Establishing initial authenticator content for any authenticators issued by the organization; Ensuring that authenticators have sufficient strength of mechanism for their intended use; Establishing and implementing administrative procedures for initial authenticator distribution, for lost or compromised or damaged authenticators, and for revoking authenticators; Changing default authenticators prior to first use; Changing or refreshing authenticators {{ insert: param, ia-05_odp.01 }} or when {{ insert: param, ia-05_odp.02 }} occur; Protecting authenticator content from unauthorized disclosure and modification; Requiring individuals to take, and having devices implement, specific controls to protect authenticators; and Changing authenticators for group or role accounts when membership to those accounts changes.

platformChecklistItems.label: Strong authenticators are available
platformChecklistItems.description: The platform must support strong authenticators such as passkeys or MFA factors.

seed.notes: Passkey support is integrated and exposed through profile management and Better Auth plugins.

evidence.title: Passkey support
evidence.description: convex/betterAuth/sharedOptions.ts enables passkey support and profile UI exposes add/delete passkey flows.

platformChecklistItems.label: Recovery events are audited
platformChecklistItems.description: Password reset and recovery events must be logged for review.

seed.notes: Recovery-related audit events are defined for reset requests and completion events.

evidence.title: Password reset audit events
evidence.description: src/lib/shared/auth-audit.ts includes password_reset_requested and password_reset_completed event types.

platformChecklistItems.label: Recovery and authenticator changes are guarded
platformChecklistItems.description: Authenticator recovery and sign-in credential changes must be constrained by rate limits or fresh-session checks.

seed.notes: Sensitive auth endpoints are rate-limited and sign-in email changes require a fresh session.

evidence.title: Better Auth recovery rate limits
evidence.description: convex/betterAuth/sharedOptions.ts applies per-route rate limits for password reset, verification email, and credential-management endpoints.

evidence.title: Fresh-session change-email protection
evidence.description: convex/betterAuth/sharedOptions.ts blocks change-email unless the session satisfies the recent step-up freshness window.

controls.customerResponsibilityNotes: Customer organizations are responsible for defining allowed credential types, approving recovery workflows, and governing exceptions to standard authentication policy.

nist80066.label: Assign a unique name and/or number for identifying and tracking user identity.
HIPAA Standard: Implement procedures to verify that a person or entity seeking access to electronic protected health information is the one claimed.

csf20.label: PR / PR.AA: Identities and credentials for authorized users, services, and hardware are managed by the organization
PR / PR.AA: Users, services, and hardware are authenticated

soc2.label: COSO Principle 10: The entity selects and develops control activities that contribute to the mitigation of risks to the achievement of objectives to acceptable levels.
COSO Principle 11: The entity also selects and develops general control activities over technology to support the achievement of objectives.
The entity implements logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures over protected information assets to protect them from security events to meet the entity's objectives.

Prior to issuing system credentials and granting system access, the entity registers and authorizes new internal and external users whose access is administered by the entity. For those users whose access is administered by the entity, user system credentials are removed when user access is no longer authorized.
The entity authorizes, designs, develops or acquires, configures, documents, tests, approves, and implements changes to infrastructure, data, software, and procedures to meet its objectives.

controls.title: Audit Record Review, Analysis, and Reporting
controls.implementationSummary: This control ensures audit records are reviewed, analyzed, and followed up through defined operational workflows. The platform provides review queues, stored review states, and integrity-linked evidence exports that support those workflows, while operator review cadence and escalation procedure remain deployment-owned.
controls.controlStatement: Review and analyze system audit records {{ insert: param, au-06_odp.01 }} for indications of {{ insert: param, au-06_odp.02 }} and the potential impact of the inappropriate or unusual activity; Report findings to {{ insert: param, au-06_odp.03 }} ; and Adjust the level of audit record review, analysis, and reporting within the system when there is a change in risk based on law enforcement information, intelligence information, or other credible sources of information.

platformChecklistItems.label: Audit review surfaces exist
platformChecklistItems.description: The platform must provide a queue or surface for reviewing audit evidence.

seed.notes: The security admin surface exposes evidence queues and review-oriented summaries.

evidence.title: Security admin review queue
evidence.description: src/routes/app/admin/security.tsx exposes control workspace and evidence review surfaces.

platformChecklistItems.label: Review records can be retained
platformChecklistItems.description: The platform should retain review status, reviewer identity, notes, and export integrity data when audit evidence is reviewed.
platformChecklistItems.verificationMethod: Evidence report schema and review workflow inspection

seed.notes: Evidence reports persist review status, notes, reviewer identity, and integrity-linked export data for later inspection.

evidence.title: Evidence report review storage
evidence.description: convex/schema.ts defines reviewStatus, reviewedAt, reviewedByUserId, reviewNotes, contentHash, and exportIntegritySummary fields for evidenceReports.

evidence.title: Evidence report review workflow
evidence.description: src/routes/app/admin/security.tsx and convex/security.ts allow reviewers to mark evidence reports reviewed or needs follow-up with notes.

platformChecklistItems.label: Operator review procedure is documented
platformChecklistItems.description: Internal review cadence and escalation expectations must be documented for platform operators.

seed.notes: No operator-owned documented review cadence or escalation procedure is attached in the repo-backed control workspace yet.

controls.customerResponsibilityNotes: Customer organizations are responsible for establishing review cadence, escalation paths, and documented follow-up for the evidence surfaced by the platform.

nist80066.label: Implement procedures to regularly review records of information system activity, such as audit logs, access reports, and security incident tracking reports.
HIPAA Standard: Implement hardware, software, and/or procedural mechanisms that record and examine activity in information systems that contain or use electronic protected health information.

csf20.label: PR / PR.PS: Log records are generated and made available for continuous monitoring
DE / DE.AE: Potentially adverse events are analyzed to better understand associated activities
DE / DE.AE: Information is correlated from multiple sources

soc2.label: The entity monitors system components and the operation of those components for anomalies that are indicative of malicious acts, natural disasters, and errors affecting the entity's ability to meet its objectives; anomalies are analyzed to determine whether they represent security events.

The entity evaluates security events to determine whether they could or have resulted in a failure of the entity to meet its objectives (security incidents) and, if so, takes actions to prevent or address such failures.

controls.title: System Monitoring
controls.implementationSummary: This control ensures the service is monitored for indicators of attack, misuse, or operationally significant security events. The platform emits monitoring-relevant signals such as scan events, audit-integrity checks, malware findings, and telemetry posture summaries, but operator alert response procedures are not yet fully evidenced in this register.
controls.controlStatement: Monitor the system to detect: Attacks and indicators of potential attacks in accordance with the following monitoring objectives: {{ insert: param, si-04_odp.01 }} ; and Unauthorized local, network, and remote connections; Identify unauthorized use of the system through the following techniques and methods: {{ insert: param, si-04_odp.02 }}; Invoke internal monitoring capabilities or deploy monitoring devices: Strategically within the system to collect organization-determined essential information; and At ad hoc locations within the system to track specific types of transactions of interest to the organization; Analyze detected events and anomalies; Adjust the level of system monitoring activity when there is a change in risk to organizational operations and assets, individuals, other organizations, or the Nation; Obtain legal opinion regarding system monitoring activities; and Provide {{ insert: param, si-04_odp.03 }} to {{ insert: param, si-04_odp.04 }} {{ insert: param, si-04_odp.05 }}.

platformChecklistItems.label: Monitoring signals are emitted
platformChecklistItems.description: The platform must emit monitoring-relevant security signals for operational review.

seed.notes: Monitoring-related platform signals are exposed through document scan events, audit integrity checks, and telemetry posture summaries.

evidence.title: Security monitoring signals
evidence.description: convex/security.ts reports document scan events, audit integrity failures, and telemetry posture.

platformChecklistItems.label: Operator alert response procedure is documented
platformChecklistItems.description: Internal alert routing and response expectations must be documented for the hosted platform.

seed.notes: No operator-owned monitoring response procedure is attached in the control workspace yet.

platformChecklistItems.label: Monitoring records are retained
platformChecklistItems.description: The platform should retain monitoring outputs that investigators or operators can review after signals are generated.

seed.notes: Monitoring-related records are retained through document scan events, audit-integrity failures, and evidence report metadata.

evidence.title: Document scan event records
evidence.description: convex/schema.ts defines documentScanEvents and convex/security.ts reports their latest status, rejection counts, and quarantine counts.

evidence.title: Audit integrity failure records
evidence.description: convex/audit.ts emits audit_integrity_check_failed events when the audit log hash chain does not verify.

controls.customerResponsibilityNotes: Customer organizations are responsible for reviewing service notifications, acting on customer-visible alerts, and integrating platform outputs into their own operational response processes.

nist80066.label: Implement procedures to regularly review records of information system activity, such as audit logs, access reports, and security incident tracking reports.

csf20.label: ID / ID.RA: Vulnerabilities in assets are identified, validated, and recorded
ID / ID.IM: Improvements are identified from evaluations
ID / ID.IM: Improvements are identified from security tests and exercises, including those done in coordination with suppliers and relevant third parties
ID / ID.IM: Improvements are identified from execution of operational processes, procedures, and activities
PR / PR.DS: The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-at-rest are protected
PR / PR.DS: The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-in-transit are protected
PR / PR.DS: The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-in-use are protected
DE / DE.CM: Networks and network services are monitored to find potentially adverse events
DE / DE.CM: External service provider activities and services are monitored to find potentially adverse events
DE / DE.CM: Computing hardware and software, runtime environments, and their data are monitored to find potentially adverse events
DE / DE.AE: Potentially adverse events are analyzed to better understand associated activities
DE / DE.AE: Information is correlated from multiple sources

soc2.label: COSO Principal 4: The entity demonstrates a commitment to attract, develop, and retain competent individuals in alignment with objectives.

The entity monitors system components and the operation of those components for anomalies that are indicative of malicious acts, natural disasters, and errors affecting the entity's ability to meet its objectives; anomalies are analyzed to determine whether they represent security events.
